title: self-contained component
text: self-contained component A remote component that prohibits the use of global objects or general variables, and some ProcScript statements and functions. A component instance of a remote component is self-contained when the component does not attempt to access global objects (global ProcScript, general variables, global variables, messages and help texts, menus, and so on) or restricted ProcScript statements. If Self-Contained is selected when a component is compiled, errors are generated for ProcScript actions that are not self-contained; otherwise, warnings are generated. An HTML skeleton is self-contained when it constitutes valid HTML 4.0. For example, all start and end tag pairs are complete. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
